identificationModule.officialTitle: Hypoxic Conditioning Combined With Exercise Training in Heart Failure Patient: a Randomised, Controled, Single Blind Study

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Heart failure impairs quality of life and exercise capacity, despite an optimal medical therapy. Alternative methods, like hypoxic conditioning coupled to exercise training, must be explored and describe
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
All heart failure patients will have a session of 45' of bicycle exercise training (in normoxia or hypoxia) AND a one hour rest session (in normoxia or hypoxia).

Exercise training sessions will consist in a continue bicycle exercise test at 70 to 80 % of the maximal heart rate, while breathing (single blind) normoxia or hypoxia gaz.

Rest conditioning consist in a one hour of rest, while breathing (single blind) normoxia or hypoxia gaz.

For hypoxia sessions, the oxygen saturation target will be fo 85-90 % for the 4 first weeks and 80-85 % for the last 4 weeks.

Three arms :

* exercise training in NORMOXIA and rest conditioning in NORMOXIA
* exercise training in HYPOXIA and rest conditioning in NORMOXIA
* exercise training in NORMOXIA and rest conditioning in HYPOXIA

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: this is a single blind study patients don't know if they are breathing normal air (normoxia) or hypoxic air (hypoxia).

armGroups.label: normoxia conditioning
armGroups.description: exercise training in normoxia and rest conditioning in normoxia

armGroups.label: exercise hypoxia
armGroups.description: exercise training in hypoxia and rest conditioning in normoxia

armGroups.label: rest hypoxia
armGroups.description: exercise training in normoxia and rest conditioning in hypoxia

interventions.name: hypoxia air
interventions.description: Normobaric hypoxia is generated by adding azote in normal air to decrease the oxygen availability.

primaryOutcomes.description: The maximal exercise capacity will be assessed by an incremental maximal bicycle exercise test
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: after 8 weeks of training and conditioning

secondaryOutcomes.measure: End diastolic left ventricle diameter in mm
secondaryOutcomes.description: In parasternal long axe, the end diastolic left ventricle diameter
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 8 weeks of training and conditioning

secondaryOutcomes.measure: systolic left ventricle ejection fraction in %
secondaryOutcomes.description: the systolic left ventricle ejection fraction will be assessed by Simpson's method in 4 and 2 cavity views
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 8 weeks of training and conditioning

secondaryOutcomes.description: The rest FMD will be assessed by echocardiography by using the % of variation of the brachial artery diameter 10 minutes after occlusion.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 8 weeks of training and conditioning

secondaryOutcomes.measure: systolic and diastolic blood pressure control in mmHg
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: After 8 weeks of training and conditioning

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* patient available for exercise training
* stable heart failure state I-II New York Heart Association (NYHA) with altered ejection fraction (EF ≤ 40 %)
* signed informed consent

Exclusion Criteria:

* unstable ischemic cardiopathy
* right ventricle dysfunction
* uncontrolled arrythmias
* rest elevated in pulmonary artery pressure (≥ 45 mmHg)
* migraine
* high altitude trip above 2500 m high during study period
* impossibility to realise a maximal bicycle exercise test
